description: Attorney edit-time in minutes — the commercial metric that decides senior-partner adoption. Lower edit-time is better; complemented by character-level edit-distance between original draft and attorney-approved version.
sub_criteria: Total edit-time in minutes from review start to ship-ready
Edit-distance (character-level) between original and approved versions

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Edit-time under [TBD] minutes; character-level edit-distance under [TBD]; ≤1 structural rewrite; no independent-claim rewrite.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Edit-time within first quartile; minor surface edits dominate; up to 1 independent-claim refinement.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Edit-time within median range; mix of structural and surface edits; 1-2 dependent-claim rewrites.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Edit-time in fourth quartile; multiple structural rewrites required; independent claim partially rewritten.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: [Pending review] Faster to draft from scratch — attorney rewrites majority of independent claim and significant spec sections.

track_b_protocol: Reviewer is timed from opening the anonymized draft to declaring ship-readiness. The edit format is [Pending: open question §16.2] — METHODOLOGY.md §16 lists this protocol as pending Sylvain + reviewer panel input. v1.0 ships placeholder; v1.1 locks the protocol.
